[{"Name":"dardmaniac1","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIzAnajELaX0Mnweu21AVEtAzI7qQbU8_seJa932Titrf2y7w=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I am at the end of the Veterinary Assisting program and from my point of view and experiences, it has been an amazing time. The cost is high, and the work is abundant in a very short amount of time, but it is easily manageable. Working full time while still attending class, I was able to complete work easily and passed every class. The hands on you get at the SPCA is priceless. The people there are great and very willing to help you learn, and the animals are very fun to work with. One of the reviews for CCC that has one star is a review made from the experience she had. I personally met her and at the time, there was some confusion with instructors and we also had an over-sized class. This caused inter turmoil between students and teachers. Please take her review into consideration, but not as a fact. Some of the students that came in at the same time as her stayed and are continuing through the program. They all seem to be enjoying it just as much as I have. If you are questioning whether to enroll in the Veterinary Assisting Program, give them a call and set up an appointment for information.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Victor Falcon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJoF_0MZc5soDAfVcvNPixDuqUe6hH-mDEAZ1mcdotrOehPdA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Missing the school some stock up people there just like everywhere.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Lyn M","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocID7yImxh5aSXYYZK8KsQSgNslqONctBFZJNg2IGxzgdG9gPg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"This facility has a nerve calling itself a “college”, notice that the majority of students are those considered “minorities”. While you can obtain basic skills that you can just pay for or actually obtain proper scholarships, this facility sells them at an extreme high price I.e. computer skills etc. What is worse is, nothing a student “learns” (despite having to pay so much after FAFSA), is that none of it is actually transferable to any proper and actual College or University. The name should be simply be switched to “program for basic skills certification school” it’s that simple.

It’s plain to see this facility simply makes money at ridiculous high amounts for basically the equivalent of individuals tutoring.

Do not go here there is nothing a student can actually transfer as far as credits to an actual College or University as the “classes” do not hold actual credit weight recognized by the board of education. You’ll end up paying more on the long run versus if you had applied to a regular public college with possibilities galore of actually being approved for scholarships. Just go to an adult school or public college.

I also had someone threatening me if I didn’t finish paying my FAFSA with them I could not attend any other college. The adaucity. When you are in college (a real college) your fafsa acts like a temporary postponement since they recongize the actual credited classes you are taking. So there you go. Research and ask questions. Many of the students that went to Central Coast “college” ended up in Hartnell and succeeded with proper credits and degrees anyway.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Maria Campos","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLysOOUsSVE6O1_ZmNoQzXACsicGF5D5YbOPkH92_oBFUP9GQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I am currently attending Central Coast College training in the Computer Specialist Accounting program. The time here has gone by so fast, i cant believe i' am almost done. The staff and teachers are amazing, they do so much, making sure you stay on track and help you one-one never leaving you behind, I' am so glad i chose to attend Central Coast College.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Esther","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLruPteVYtXSRv3P6bv5Ni1ddHXXzlk0YIv7bd5KRSOpb1f3g=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I decided to attend Central Coast College because I heard many great things about it. This made me realize I had an opportunity that I could not reject. I am currently enrolled, and all I can say is that this is an awesome college, and the instructors are great!!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Destiny Valdez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI1RiWzy6-sK8eJ0j7nqoAwyT-yDrpUZbNmLI3H85acmek2rQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"This is the worst school you could ever attend . When I first started they made it seem like such a great school and they are there to help their students get an education, but in all reality they are just a money hungry school that screws you over once you’ve completed the program. When they make mistakes they try to make it seem like it’s your fault and “oh well” you have to deal with it and not them. Oh and Mr. Schaefer is the rudest, money hungry, most unprofessional person I have ever met! He needs to seriously learn how to communicate with other people ! I would never recommend this school to anyone else ! I was told by one of their employees last Wednesday that I can participate in graduation ceremony this winter and was showed my petition for graduation and it was all completely signed off and I was ready to go . Now today only like 5 days since I was told this now all of a sudden I’m unable to participate! Like seriously this school always comes up with something ! They are full of it ! Honestly at this point I don’t even care to participate in their graduation ! I don’t want to be apart of this crooked money hungry school any longer ! People don’t screw yourselves over and attend this school !","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Terrell Turinia","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKz60JpXp39Rff0mItq2Uir6uu45dnXKSQ6WcfXFVpcYKpXVw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Central Coast College was the best decision I've ever made. My life was at a stand still and I had no idea which direction to go. My job skills were out of date by 10 years. My self esteem and self confidence were at an all time low. Once I made the decision to attend the Computer Office Administration course the office staff and teachers took care of the rest. The financial aid department assisted me with all the FASFA and student loan process. The teachers and class mates made this 9 month program fun and challenging. There was always support when I needed it. I learned a tremendous amount in a short time. I am more than prepared to take on the world, with the sky being the limit.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Jaqueline F. Medel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUQa_rYyeb83K3kqZ4pojOWHsydIvGoTaRXqbvRi3U4OUs5N1SlLg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Coming to CCC is been one of the best choices I ever made.  Yes, it was a little challenging but never impossible.  Most of the instructors are always there to help you out and give you the tools you need to succeed in the real world.  I honestly believe that if you really want something, there won't be boundaries to prevent you from nothing... if you think you are not getting the right tools in class, go and talk to the directors and believe me, they will make the changes.  Before I even finished the Computer Specialist Accounting program, I got hired by an institution owned by the Monterey County and I couldn't be happier (starting my career).  So if you really wish for something, don't expect CCC to do everything for you, YOU GET THE TOOLS, DO YOUR HOMEWORK, LOOK FOR THE CAREER YOU WANT, AND APPLY, APPLY, APPLY, DON'T GIVE UP AND ALWAYS BELIEVE IN YOURSELF.  So take advantage of the tools that CCC gives you and put them to work.","Images":null,"When":""}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Teah Amanda","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUX6Q0UTTkW35UGWwmGVRSJ5N0gj3pWJpzcQzUOE6cmpgcgTxEw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Save your time and money—don’t fall victim like I did. The organization is lacking and their prices are inflated. They charge thousands for "materials" that only cost $50 to $100. Where is the rest of the financial aid going?

Good luck navigating financial aid on your own; there's little support. When you’re down to your last year, they suddenly expect you to pay thousands after maxing out your aid—information you might not even have received until it’s too late. Now you can’t continue your studies because you can’t afford the $3,000 out-of-pocket.

It’s a scam and a joke.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Joseph Cluett","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXsxxFR4SCrPSNZMrUdzxsljmozhj7S9MKNf2SAc2a4xZmSgK-C=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"This school has ridiculously poor communication. I have been trying to contact someone for weeks. I have called every number listed at several different campuses and I literally have received voicemails every time. I finally talked to an admissions counselor, was told to send my transcripts for review and now it is almost 10 days later and haven't heard from him since. I contacted another school and it is like night and day! Greeted on the first call, regular follow ups, able to be reached, etc.. You would think that you would get better service considering the fact that your posting thousands of dollars. I decided I don't even want to go to Cambridge College after all now.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sally Jackson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKSTUvrdXtuueQGc610WB_6E-KeWnYtaaOArM3YMwCjxI1EIg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"I will make my long story short and get to the point. If I can save another victim from from attending this school, its worth my time in writing this. As soon as they knew my loan was approved, I was told everything I wanted to hear so I could attend this school and they could steal my money for a degree that was worthless to me. I wanted to get a masters degree in child dev. but since they did not offer the degree, I was advised to pursue a masters degree in Interdisciplinary studies. I was lied to and told that this degree would work for me because it was in education. I trusted them and was stupid enough to fall for their lies. 23,000 dollars later, I come to realize that this degree was useless! I've been denied many opportunities because employers tell me that my degree isn't specialized in anything. When I contacted them to plead my case, they acted as If they had done no wrong. The president of Cambridge ignored my calls! I wrote a formal complaint to their accreditation and they were in the school's favor. I explained that I was not even offered an orientation!!!! They turned a blind eye! The classes are mediocre and people that can't even speak or write in the English language can literally buy their Masters Degree! What a joke!! So, please don't say you were not warned about this school. The previous academic director quit her position and admitted to me that Cambridge does have unethical practices! Imagine that! They offered no resolution for what they did. Go to another school that offers a real valuable education. I wish I had been warned! This mistake cost me heartbreak and serious debt of 23,000 dollars! Now I have to go back to school and get a real degree.  Stay away!!!!!! These fly by night schools are not worth it!!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Juana Mancera","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL33EvKoKwWmFtGPYPK9I5TutgY4PR1iaO8sUncgjfASYusbg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""}]

[{"Name":"Alex Holler","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKTm3_ye8dkrWC7ctenW9fcYnohyFw1cSJvGt5MuK1n7GxdkQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"As a masters student who came in from the workforce I could tell all of my professors had heaps of industry experience. This was such a healthy departure from my undergrad experience at a big state school where academics focus on themselves. Many members of staff at USV are genuinely interested in the success and understanding of their students.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Mihail Grumeza (MishaTheAudioGuy)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXH0TMcfLIUuc8P3Uiy3Rw5eQVfXDbWlbJmj_HJvWV_kw6EAXBy=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I just graduated here.
High tuition for untreated "studios" which are just rooms.
Old outdated studio monitors. Broken/ sometimes working sound board. Broken gear in the AV lab thats never been replaced. False promises on fixing and upgrading studios for years. False marketing when recruiting students. Seems like they only care about money and lie about delivering top of the line equipment.
One semesters tuition can buy you a better studio than what they offer here. Even most of the teachers are not full time and have to cancel class due to a second job.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Josiah Clark","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVlSSceIG7L7SZtW9gTZI9XoCtsRPTE9JDyRBiV02SdyADBAB_5Fg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The way I feel about USV, it is what you make it. The school requires you to advocate for yourself and take advantage of the things they offer. I can't speak for the other majors but when it came to Game Writing I was given exactly what I would need to be a narrative designer in the industry. When switching over to the industry other than getting used to the proprietary engine and just the flow of working with people, it felt no different than how I felt in my writing courses.

I will say there's a handful of teachers that really made staff that made this school be exactly what I needed. Professor Skolnick, Carlous, Jason, Phil, all made my time in USV the perfect training ground for me.

Why I didn't give the school a perfect score it's mainly cause I have minor gripes with the school that wasn't really in control of these individuals. From the tech just needing to be more modern to certain classes just me desiring more from it. Specifically the Game Design courses, though while I was there they were slowly progressing the course and it was definitely trending better.  If you love a small school, with some great teachers and great staff that will push you as far as you desire, this is the school for you. But you have to be prepared to put your own work in.

Also as those mentioned the price is really out of this world. And for some that is a very rightful deterrent.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Alexis Lujan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWeCFUf8SYahWjaQl17z1fjud8Ndum3J0bQWCBP7v6cJNQR8DTxCQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"I want to make this clear, I enjoyed the time I had at the college. I gain knowledge of 3D modeling and got me into the proper lane of success artistically for 3D modeling (Love ya Monica Cappiello, you were awesome!) But, they're sudden changes in this year that left me flabbergasted and upset. Please have a open mind about reading this review. I was once a student at the college.

2015, I had fun! I did great and I really took time to know my surroundings, the new campus at the time and met some really awesome friends. I learned a bunch of 3D modeling and my passion awaken, making me feel so determine to keep going and to learn more ways on how to model anything. Learning the tools I need as well as getting professional feedback from the professors as well.

Then, 2018 rolls in.
Tours, Tours and MORE tours.
Rose up the tuition, had a huge leak in the college from the water heater and decided to do an Art show while the building was still in construction.

Oh, there's more. They pushed back my graduation because I didn't want to take Modeling 2. The class was a huge joke, and also a scam too because it wasn't anything like my Modeling 1 class, but it was all about tools that were for ONE model only and it was a stupid car. No characters, no environments, no lessons on shaders, no texture techniques, nothing. Just a car and maybe work in Unreal Engine. I didn't go to school to just have my progress halted by some professor who decided that his class was just one big "here's tools and uh, we're modeling a car so that's what y'all." How dumb and embarrassing, I'm paying to learn not to be in a joke class.

Teachers now these days are making certain classes have these outlandish and YET ridiculous times such as 7pm to 9pm for a night-time class. And most who don't live in housing can't commute to that perfectly well. They're planning to move to different campuses within a year so newer students won't be used to the San Jose Building, pretty typical. Where's the acceptance rates, Cogswell? It was 53 to 63 to --? Really? Hiding it now because you have 10+ students leaving and it's messing up the rates? No kidding. And the parking is a nightmare.

I wouldn't suggest this school in a heart beat, even the touring will be stressful to get to, where would you park? Anyways, this college has gone down pretty badly and I would keep supporting it if they would of fixed these issues such as the crowding issue. But I know they won't fix any of this. They won't listen to students like us, they wouldn't care if anyone becomes homeless after the money they owe back to the school and the jobs they won't be able to even find.

PLEASE, consider talking to previous students like me who went to this school. I was a DAA student who wanted to do 3D modeling, I'm planning to do it solo and for commission work online until I manage to build my own portfolio up and find a job myself. Don't take this college unless you ask questions, I wish I asked more questions but hence, I was naive in 2015. Don't make the same mistake as me.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Rhognald McDognald's","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXCopEVEuIfJxEx9nK3CcZnh3oEdIrzvyPmewtRiDcSv4fg_z4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"While attending Cogswell my car was broken into while parked on campus and my final project was stolen from a computer lab. After graduating i was unable to find work in my field. Now unable to pay my tuition debt i teeter on the verge of homelessness. I suggest  you choose another educational option. Attending Cogswell was the single biggest mistake I made in life.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"J","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVqhgtUyQ3Gj5flNewPwvT6hXwsDnd6ikpWG0zQBMs3hAdqNBIq0g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Very lackluster. Administration was cordial, teachers a mixed bag and student body can be at large unpleasant. For the cost of admission to their recent move i have found it all utterly unacceptable.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Alori","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJpLkO6xfDF4OwsnR1-U90-c-q9M0M4oENXbu_QO93URA0r6iN3=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Not a real school","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"B. [{"Name":"Philip LeNoble","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXy5yXta0TrUV8Tji1lilpseL_itfOhPJfkjT3BgoRpDgP6Z2y1=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"As my national media consultancy grew in the early 80s, and I was traveling 46 weeks a year, I was able to gain a wonderful and extended way in a distance learning opportunity to enroll in an M.B.A. program at California Coast University. The complete course work took me from 1984-1987 and was richly rewarding being similar to top Universities at that time when distance learning and degree programs were still scant.
From 1987 to 1993, I was able to add another vital piece of education to my portfolio, a Ph.D. in Management. I had great help from Dr Philip Borden and Dr. David Smith as I successfully defended my dissertation.
I am truly grateful for the great and challenging opportunity I had with a terrific and challenging curriculum from the University. And, for other executives in the making, this was an awesome experience to be able to finish my educational  objectives and goal!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Jen A","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJzkH3u3hfD9o2mo0q71BDV_o-Rg0KVKZP4qgo-z2t4WDc6Ew=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I have been enrolled in a doctorate program at California Coast for over a year. This is the best educational experience I have ever had, and I have 4 other graduate degrees. I work and I have several children under age 10, this is completely doable with a busy schedule. I love learning at my own pace with no busy work. No. Busy. Work. My last doctorate degree required multiple weekly forum posts that took 10 hours per week and gave no benefit, in addition to papers and exams. With California Coast, none of my time is wasted. The textbook choices are thoughtful and the exams are well-crafted. I have learned so much and I am enjoying it. The professors provide honest, constructive feedback. It is a phenomenal price, especially if your employer will setup a corporate partnership, and the monthly payments are very doable. I have already recommended California Coast to several colleagues and family members.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Louis Sewell","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKyurWO7ABvidB2lo-yiOBZrFgUig_kFOgCr6ppOifNHUqVwA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Great school for someone that has to juggle a full time job and school. I graduated with a bachelors degree in 2014 and it's opened more opportunities for me. Nowadays employers just want a bachelors degree no matter what the field is because to them it proves that you're willing to put in the time and effort to accomplish a greater goal. Having my bachelors degree from CCU has boosted my confidence and career salary by $15,000. Everything is self study at your own pace which I really like as opposed to a quarter/semester system with traditional colleges. The professors are helpful should you have questions and tuition/books are really affordable. If you take shortcuts learning in your education you have no one else to blame but yourself. A degree can get you the interview but it's your knowledge, experiences, and presentation that'll get you the job.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Russell J. Moon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXSL0UOTKF3Mn7E2BiMjVcsViiE0IGZx85wM6f1r3sIQlsZXyY9=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Because of work schedule commitments, there was no way to continue my education in the traditional brick & mortar classroom environment.  I have been a student online for my Associates Degree at Kaplan University, my Bachelor's at Baker College and most recently for my MBA at CCU.  Every program has it strengths, but I appreciate the ability to drive my education largely at my own pace allowing me to timely finish classes when it fits into my schedule.

As an MBA graduate and now a Doctoral student at CCU, I would never continue my education at this level at a school I did not have 100% confidence in.  The professors are fast with grading and question response, as well is the staff.  They are always willing to do their utmost to help or assist a student.  Many times they go above and beyond what one would expect.

For potential students, you really must evaluate if online learning is for you, if you need teachers and students to fuel your motivation level, ANY online college may not be for you; but if you can motivate yourself and know online learning is for you, don't hesitate!

I am a veteran and there has never been an issue with their benefits for veterans, take advantage of what you have earned.  Busy adults with full-time jobs, you can do it too, you decide what, where, and when you can complete the work, especially if you know the pain of having to participate in a chatroom at a specific time, not here; you are in control of your destiny, as education should be!

CCU is one of the most affordable accredited colleges I have researched, and I did a lot before deciding to go back to school...again.  If you have any questions, ask them, the staff are excellent and leave nothing to chance, this University has done nothing more than expanded my career options, knowledge base and prospects.  If you want a degree, you should go and get it!

Don't be fooled though, online learning can be harder than traditional learning and it may take you a little time to get used to it, but don't suffer needlessly, call the school, ask for assistance, see what they can offer and work with you on.  Too many people complain they feel alone in their education, but nothing could be further from the truth, they are letting you be responsible for your education!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Greg Hargrove","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXgrcMA3FLEaSIUmjv_ku13_2eQoU0pyGEJBcGRskJUSuWtQxc5=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I earned my Psychology degree at California Coast University. While the curriculum was rigorous, I felt supported by the faculty and staff at every step in the process. My questions were always answered promptly and in detail.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Alex O","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKfd-FxAVQeavh7K5FtGm3AHGFuJAWNe3htF2ZkWWLX-wuUeQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I’m currently enrolled in the MBA program with a focus on HR management. Amazing program for a working adult with a family. I have learned a ton in this program and i can’t say enough about the price and the fact it’s paced. This is very doable! CCU has made it easy !","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Teresa G.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW1lZqwRfIZCYwgy_GD7Bud1GGc6Q-oP3g0CjpjY1G1st8zmghb=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"California Coast University has given me the flexibility to achieve my dreams and goals. I’m in the Bachelor of Science in Management (BS) degree program. Everyone is super helpful & encouraging. They want me to succeed.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Rick Suttle","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXpFR0Mu68YF3ASGOosKLvxRrBKHHR6lIQUfSNf-K8mbI0E0Xg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I graduated with an MBA in 2000.  I was living in Lexington, Kentucky at the time and bought my textbooks from the main University of Kentucky campus store. The flexible program fit in well with my job and I learned a lot. CCU is truly what you make of the experience.","Images":null,"When":""}]

title: Santa Clara University
address: 500 El Camino Real, Santa Clara, CA 95053, United States
descriptions: This 1851 Jesuit university offers a scenic campus with gardens & Mission Revival-style buildings.
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Agnieszka “Aki” Stepinska","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVoyBqaPFpaCOldCu15hapJygU9vXFIEEgz9LCFsPHIZEQQDiICFA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Santa Clara University
A really nice campus. Beautiful buildings, well maintained and very clean. It has a historic feel with plenty of architectural buildings to appreciate. We didn’t go inside any of them just walked around campus. There is an old church that may be worth a look. You can walk-on campus but to visit the campus by car you have to get a visitor parking pass either by the entry or go to the security booth. The security were very nice and will give you a map of the campus.

The campus exudes a sense of affluence, and it's evident that there's wealth here. While walking around, we came accross a teacher's meeting were all the teachers were very well dressed. They looked more like professionals in corporate attire than your typical brainy educators. The neighborhood around the campus is well-maintained and felt safe. The campus atmosphere felt friendly but also a bit preppy. Despite that, the campus is definitely worth a visit, as it's beautiful.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipNLIdx2jIfH76n564rWBcTjYkpPc1xMh3pN7O_o&fid=0x0:0x561ca114f6d4e347"],"When":"2023-4-16"},{"Name":"Ray Torres","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWqvcisDq4tzpgHHMxZAg626q-vjk6SoS0YM8r6y7lqJZoJmvA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Walking the grounds was tranquil and serene. One of the most beautiful UC campuses. Many paths, statues and squirrels 🐿️! The Santa Clara mission is not far and a theater that has worth while events. Well kept grounds, even to have lunch at the bench! All of this and within city limits.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipPI9UJ7gS-n1X3Z7iqE56B6B0ZXl6dPGQRzd1SU&fid=0x0:0x561ca114f6d4e347"],"When":"2022-12-27"},{"Name":"Adrian Zhang","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXbYUV--tWZCfg68QaVBjg-8PJjFZlRE1NTbiekNZzqOXRT0sI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"i have attended SCU for two years and it has simply sucked all the joy out of my life. i struggle to find a reason to go on. would not really recommend going here, to be honest. not even if you are the sad, mopey type.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Lisa Elley","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUyKh1ewZdpbqZoUxla9qX3wutLW-d1lenSoEONV1eKHfty9Wy9kg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Gorgeous campus, I come walking here every day. Gardens and flower beds are immaculate. Love it. There’s bathrooms by the parking lot on Lafayette as well as next to the science building. They’ve been mostly locked over Covid and distance learning. Security guards on campus are usually patrolling around, it’s well lit with emergency phones. Don’t use the vending machine by the science building, it’s been faulty for years lol. There’s a Safeway close by but you can’t bring a picnic and linger if you’re not a student. You can walk around and use the benches etc and take photos. If you use a professional photographer they’ll ask for a permit. The art museum is free and worth a visit. It’s been closed with Covid though.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipOe-ExpOpCNj-z40vIC_WWWYHEMfXibtcjQW9Fk&fid=0x0:0x561ca114f6d4e347"],"When":"2021-11-9"},{"Name":"Elaine Chan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKSd1JWdK_dWVNJxTp9ZgsKwoc6Xzl4bjk6uSXuLOWrKMQgUA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A quite and peaceful university
There is a church located at the heart of the university
Heard that a lot of couples will have their wedding ceremony in this church “Mission Santa Clara de Asís”
You can find a lot of flowers, trees and squirrels in this university 🐿️😆
The squirrels are so cute! Some will even walk toward you 😂","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipMqV42Y-0jiOT8zs0tIr6g7mBNzeMG7mivY9XbO&fid=0x0:0x561ca114f6d4e347"],"When":"2022-10-24"},{"Name":"Johannes Widodo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV9WhaCsxDE-TJYogvyZEICS7Rw8g9DlXQWhPkjE04yVoOSMPUTYw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very nice campus, international students","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipMISXGvrj_p43TQcafD_-LWgSLiQnHmPtxINfuz&fid=0x0:0x561ca114f6d4e347"],"When":"2023-6-18"},{"Name":"HW","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV8nYoefVh3iUQSpwkm-YVfEt7XBuPBlkB1jQoeyw_bQDM4zQz5pA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wonderful campus, easy access, park at streets, especially to see wisteria flowers on that pathway!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipMq1v8GMkFPZv-70gPPSYP_oK6fE4FPT5MtKYj8&fid=0x0:0x561ca114f6d4e347"],"When":"2022-3-23"},{"Name":"Brian D. Lee","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXTgvJ7XcsCdD90wdOEbiUoPMt6Z5uV1tvI6WQ9RUmwmOPlwnM=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"During my visit to Santa Clara University, I was impressed by its blend of modern and historic architecture, creating a welcoming atmosphere. The campus's well-equipped facilities and serene outdoor spaces left a lasting impression of Santa Clara University's holistic approach to education.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipORQuAUv1jjqrFgTNXtKlAsRAxy6QG6d5DRvYKz&fid=0x0:0x561ca114f6d4e347"],"When":"2023-8-17"}]

user_reviews: [{"Name":"Daniel Alamilla","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUqIi6a07kC7ec8ngxkZc_O6L6KHQThNci07YhRg075SCQ0pRuEvA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One of the best places in the country to start or continue your professional career. Love the campus and the staff. Every staff member and instructor is very professional and kind.  A wide variety of programs in many fields.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipPySmF1KW3Llivtq1VaIn4f2_1NSGh5QmnlqpjK\u0026fid=0x0:0xf61ca9c09d4ebbde"],"When":"2022-10-17"},{"Name":"ahhh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUexslNaiUS1VVuGk2FWpaM7Ih4Kqsf1wJwfAsZ234Jwt4_0sHu=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Fresno City College definitely has it's flaws, but honestly, ever college (even major UCs) are too. As far as CC goes, this place is incredibly vibrant. The campus is really beautiful especially with all the older brick buildings. There is a HUGE social life here (especially for CC standards). Asian Fest in the Spring, a public Halloween festival for family and kids. A public pride event for pride month. A game room with billiard ball tournaments and giant cafeteria for socializing and doing homework. Honestly for anyone looks for CC in Fresno area, I would recommend this place 100% over Clovis or Madera. This college even has a 100+ person lecture hall.\n\nLike any college it has flaws, there are some horrible teachers here and to be honest, the Computer Science teachers here are atrocious. But as a summary, Fresno City is the only community college in the area that has aspects that makes it feel like a real four year college.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Steven","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX1V5rg4igoqrkr-sEhYV8cmzuzPJkqFvou4bAzmLh-zzIyJHa7rw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I went to this college in 2020 before COVID happened and I took a conducting class with Alicia Wells and a composition class with Mike Dana and I loved them but too bad the semester was cut short","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Martin Borjas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXobI7qOigYnIVLfILu3ej6_dd0V4SCniOrl5LXche-_aohaCo=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Radiology program process is absurd. Anyone who goes into medical program already knows the chances of getting in on the first try is extremely lucky and difficult. There program is lottery driven and only offered every TWO YEARS. So if you applied 3 times starting in 2023 and still have not got in, it would be 2029! I’ve researched many other programs within the state and so far FCC is the only program that opens every two years.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Felipe","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVOmJ4EQCw_NKYdAvXWcyY_ND6SjmG1SfO5KLVCXGbCushYA-o=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"It's an open campus so it is interesting to see many different types of people here. Always make sure to stay on top of your agenda and fees because their system is pretty sloppy.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"kevin rangel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVCcXoazc9HocLQVp9wchBgLgZzMI_7k7RsEL8KZAAMlQgqWw27Hw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very popular college. We weren't allowed inside any of the buildings because of COVID rules, but it was still an interesting tour.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipNq1QaqJLnQZskt2Fgb1Qi72Rqg4DkKFEhYAV5R&fid=0x0:0x3499e7d01a61dd1a"],"When":"2022-6-7"},{"Name":"Mary Born","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUCwT4EY8TClxqu7-kPBAzCyePSG8DvKJS9I8vp7BgQjYqhmPY=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"California Institute Of Technology is the greatest learning institution on the planet. My personal experience with doctors, teachers, students, alumni and researchers is extraordinary.

The campus is beautiful and massive. Located in a prestigious area of South Pasadena, the campus is home to sprawling buildings, gardens, laboratories and understated California architectural masterpieces.

If you decide to tour the campus, check out the turtle ponds, the library and the newest additions to the campus, neuroscience and molecular biology research centers.

I always get a special feeling walking around the campus having been exposed to the work being done here, big breakthroughs in science, math, medicine and technology happen every day. This university is beautiful, but what's inside, and what comes out of here, is extraordinary.

If your mission is changing the world for the better, finding cures, searching for the answers to life's greatest challenges, and saving lives, visit, register, study and volunteer at Cal Tech.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipMRjmWIhXgjUKLLR8OTnjHn2JjkNXNzZWUDQaXI&fid=0x0:0x3499e7d01a61dd1a"],"When":"2022-5-18"},{"Name":"Georgine Okura","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLPQ0REIn0VereOjxtmQDZsHc1zMtvlem1Xfjw4qylbNFUlqw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Beautiful and peaceful campus.  Many non-Caltech students/staff enjoying the grounds on a daily basis.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipO7gfRrL39RRp8oOSDa86-IS5Hy8FFs-axuoCIV&fid=0x0:0x3499e7d01a61dd1a"],"When":"2022-1-7"},{"Name":"অ আ ক খ","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLNAH2Mg9xVlO9U_gumbRdOeVhR2tWLkEhKw1ne72e13-xemqok=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The California Institute of Technology (Caltech) is one of the world’s leading science and engineering research institutions, located in Pasadena, California. Established in 1891, Caltech is renowned for its rigorous academic programs, groundbreaking research, and high faculty-to-student ratio. It focuses primarily on science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) disciplines, with a strong emphasis on fostering innovation and scientific discovery.

Caltech has a small student body, with around 1,000 undergraduates and 1,300 graduate students, ensuring personalized attention and close interaction between students and faculty. The institute's faculty includes Nobel laureates, Turing Award winners, and Fields Medalists, highlighting the exceptional caliber of its teaching and research staff.

The institution is also home to several advanced research facilities, including the Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), which is managed for NASA, and the Caltech Seismological Laboratory, a leader in earthquake research. Caltech's contributions to space exploration, quantum computing, and sustainability science have shaped modern scientific and technological progress.

Beyond academics, Caltech cultivates a collaborative and close-knit community, encouraging interdisciplinary work and hands-on problem-solving. The Honor Code, a key part of campus life, emphasizes trust and ethical behavior, empowering students to take responsibility for their learning and conduct.

Caltech’s reputation as a premier research institution continues to attract top-tier students and researchers from around the world, fostering an environment of curiosity, excellence, and creativity.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Alva J Gillis","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXeqfM2HQyeXiHrHOFluhq-A47ut-sLSEmKTWkiIFB_zkjLgIKLxQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The researchers zeroed in on one segment in particular, which runs from southern Vancouver Island alongside Washington state, more or less ending at the Oregon border. The subterranean topography of other segments is relatively rough, with oceanic features like faults and subducted seamounts rubbing up against the upper plate—features that might erode the upper plate and limit how far any quake may pronate within the segment, thus limiting the quake’s size. In contrast, the Vancouver-Washington segment is quite smooth.","Images":null,"When":""}]

DR.Kim , the professor, who ironically teaches ethics, and it was the same with other classes that she was teaching has consistently demonstrated some of the most unethical behavior I've encountered in an academic setting.

From the start, it was clear that she had little regard for fair treatment or academic integrity. Her grading system was arbitrary and inconsistent, often favoring certain students without clear justification. Requests for clarification or feedback were met with hostility or outright dismissal. Furthermore, she was frequently unprepared for lectures, often recycling outdated material without any effort to update or engage with current ethical debates.

Her office hours were practically inaccessible, and on the rare occasion when she was available, she was unapproachable and unhelpful. Many students, including myself, felt belittled and disrespected during interactions with her. It became evident that she had little interest in fostering an environment of learning or ethical inquiry.

This experience has severely tarnished my view of the program and the university as a whole. I would caution prospective students to think twice before enrolling at Cal State LA, especially if they value ethical teaching and academic integrity. This professor's conduct is not only a personal disappointment but also a glaring reflection on the university's standards and oversight. This experience has severely tarnished my perception of the program and the university as a whole. It's not a massive campus but not small either. It takes 10-15 minutes to walk from side to side. Restaurants, cafes and other food and drink places are all around campus, with 3-4 starbucks cafes in and around the campus. The new construction of the USC village has made such a positive contribution to the whole ambience of the campus. Then outside the campus is Golleher Alumni House where my uncle David's wedding was at. He and Randy got married on September 6, 2008.

Other than that, the campus is so beautiful even when it was founded in 1957 with the mascot Tuffy the Titan which is an elephant. It was summertime, so the campus was not crowded. We were impressed with the beautiful buildings and the overall atmosphere of the campus. There was background music playing from speakers all around, adding to the ambiance.

The buildings looked well taken care of, with nice seating areas throughout the campus. Few students were moving about on these fast scooters as the campus is vast. There was a lot of trees and plants all around - very green campus.

What stood out to us were the unique architectural features on many buildings, which made it different from the other three schools we visited that day. It was a very pretty and fun visit for a family of a prospective student.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipPVGJFZreQwrVyPkcU7Vp6CVkNpE51WPKEmYz-i&fid=0x0:0x2b9810bbb94af355"],"When":"2023-7-20"},{"Name":"Abdull Tarzi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWrTcHJ5VgFYNi6v2OOH1LQjBlC0y_dfbErgGm4zjJRKAQcqt2iJA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This wad best university in world. Another teacher was good music teacher and showed us history from music from 1500 to present and some musicians had little or no money to survive yet the music was one most important thing for human in past present and may be in future.  Going to university was happy to myself because I had very good looking cars and very good looking girls 2 talked to me and I wonder how I can find the 2 beautiful girls from Cal State Fullerton. Abdull  Tarzi","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"JAS “Mr.J62” Lin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKJMh7ktLmkcL5bMUOgtZnroGhliP57H5gK7neeaf535JTNIA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Idk much about other program but their business programs for undergraduate are pretty underrated compared to the UC level university. I also take a couple general courses for upper division from other departments, just as good as the business they tend to promote. They have a pretty solid journalism program as they will give you a practical experience by working as a reporter or any support for their own independent newspaper. Wish I could do more as a business undergrad but was a struggling student financially just like many. I will always cherish my experience as I have met some of the best professor in their field of interest along with other students that become a friend still until now. It’s a good value undergrad university for tuitions and such. The main issue I notice is the many fees they introduced while you’re there and yeah Parking, like many people have mentioned. Still, it’s a real university with some of the best people you would encounter. There are many universities out there offering dubious, barely accredited programs especially on the private university. Went for MonkeyPox vaccine and they also do corona in their parking lot 🅿️","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipOohOu7TXQm2lM5UFexSww2FAlSl7jczGjCpTPu&fid=0x0:0x436a6bd31c932d38"],"When":"2022-11-10"},{"Name":"Richard J","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXsi6i6bTyr8v6fcjtvbczwnSRQ9bJnqPvobQebnPSUjsvYpi4=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I am writing to express my deep frustration with Canada College, based on an experience that started in 2018. Despite my commitment as a student, I encountered a bewildering administrative issue that has persisted for years.

In 2018, I was surprisingly and mistakenly academically suspended over a class I never registered for. This suspension significantly disrupted my academic progress. After years of effort, the suspension was finally lifted, and I successfully completed the course with a 90% grade.

However, the complications didn't end there. Despite my high score in the class, I was informed that my GPA was still considered too low to re-register for the 2024 semester. This decision seemed to disregard my recent academic achievement.

Moreover, the wrongful suspension has left a 'W' mark on my academic record, which has been challenging to rectify. In my interactions with the administration, particularly with Edith Flores, I found the response to be extremely rude and condescending. A woman like this should be scrubbing toilets. Her attitude added to my frustration and disappointment with the institution.

This prolonged struggle over the past six years has been incredibly stressful and disheartening. It's a situation that no student should have to endure. I hope that by sharing my experience, Canada College will address these administrative challenges to prevent future students from facing similar difficulties.

Including specific details about interactions can be helpful, but remember to keep the language respectful to ensure your feedback is taken seriously.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Juliana B. user_reviews: [{"Name":"why parceti","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKwXWHshXvOV3VbSVUcGe-nis_yAG_8JdUw6KbWnIkO15sRCQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I went here about 14 years ago, close to when this school started… looking back it’s hard not to get emotional about this place.  Though I was only there a couple semesters, I made connections and friends that I have to this day.  All the teachers were very skilled and their range was incredible.  They could go from mainstream pop to complex metal or jazz.   Their drum teacher was on countless mo-town records, as was the engineer teacher who was also on Michael Jackson records, among many others.   He took us to  a private event at the Beverly Hills hotel with legends like Quincy jones, and Barry Gordy in attendance.  The other engineer teacher was a talented drummer and had roots very deep in LA underground hip hop.  The class settings are intimate and sometimes intense. you will be pushed. you will get better. This school was overall a surreal and invaluable time of my life and absolutely unforgettable.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"David Aranda","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUFWoQLPLL8MV45NYLLuTQqyaXsD6wYDF2oDEwcC0ZkTIN6U5xB=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This school is a great opportunity to take your musical abilities to the next abilities. [{"Name":"Hyunje Jo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUdtIHKngu4EgIKuTgtfYTCG08OOmaR_thd-mroVi0zWiX0jNi2=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Located in the Santa Clara area of California in the United States, the California Institute of the Arts was formed by the merger of the Chouinard Art Institute, which was established in 1921, and the Los Angeles Conservatory of Music, which was established in 1883, and is an art colleage where art and music coexist.

The alumni association was founded in 1965 by Walt Disney's younger brother, Roy Disney, and is now considered the most prestigious college with the most expensive tuition and the best professors, faculties and graduates in the field.

There are majors of Art & Technology,
Program in Art, Photography and Media, and Graphic Design.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipPxUd8bdQqY_Pm55N3P9sOTWjeXak2kXOnnXJKW&fid=0x0:0x446f87ec0d840bc8"],"When":"2024-6-8"},{"Name":"Bahar Tahani","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWgSV8H1XcxK8gt6HLWzg0WqqRFpNV0RwjG3TI1gG5jpIKT02Dn=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"MFA student in Art Program. user_reviews: [{"Name":"Jesse Chan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWwTrf76Y9vFyXptrIU1esi_3ITOUtJEQZPtLh4KKlo22qFnc6v=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"\"Crown has a favorable reputation for academically-focused students.\" - Wikipedia\nAfter one academic year at Crown College, I now understand why. Unlike some other colleges where some \"party people\" hold large parties, stuck in drugs and produce insane noises all-night, it is quiet here at Crown 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Thanks to Crown's strong academic roots, I can feel a great atmosphere of learning everywhere. Crown Library is one of the few learning places on campus which opens 24/7 (official hours are 8AM-2AM but practically you can stay as long as you want). I had to call several times just to try and reach someone that seemed to know what they were talking about and every time I called I was given different information. Just because every international student decides to stay in a homestay doesn't mean I want to.

Honestly, having the audacity to accept new students without informing them of WHERE THEY ARE GOING TO LIVE is disgusting, especially when you are accepting new applicants until May 1st (They also said make the decision BY May 1st? What do they want?). If you're an international student, do not go here. Instead, go to Long Beach, any Poly university, Fullerton, or Fresno. title: California College of the Arts (CCA)
address: 145 Hooper St, San Francisco, CA 94107, United States
plus_code: QJ92+69 Showplace Square, San Francisco, CA, USA
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Jessica Ayran","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWEl8658vwkn-vIj_Y6j06vW0DlB2ECRE5k5fSYvwvUBJaGBZsx=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"CCA is a solid art school, imo. I attended from ‘11-‘16 and 75% don’t completely regret it. It’s very expensive, however you have access to great facilities, new technologies/machines, and talented creatives. Teachers are young over-achievers that inspire and grow alongside the incoming bright minds that pass through these halls. Architecture is difficult and you technically kind of learn everything on your own. I recommend learning basic building construction and drafting before arriving. Here, it’s all about experimentation and exploring new forms/ideologies or questioning the conventional. Also, materials are expensive. [ for the mid-class or lower (like myself), I recommend ordering drafting/tech specs beforehand online (Aliexpress, Newegg/FB marketplace). ]","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Gabriela Saez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLr-vbe6Iq39nxfi52ok0Yv58XVrSJ0Wh41NfHCNj--k1EIqw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"If I could give them a minus star, I would be happy to do that. Do not waste your time nor your money, this so called "art school" is good for nothing. From teachers to admins, everything is a constant unpleasant situation to deal with. Teachers really DO NOT TEACH anything, students have to find out  and learn everything mostly by themselves;  teachers are stuck up, they thing they are the biggest deal and that the students are nothing unless they get to have a favorite one. None of the students here study and work, most of them come from rich families that pay for them to do "something in life" and that something will certainly be nothing because hardly one gets to have a decent, paying job after being ripped off for years.

For rich people this is certainly not important but not for poor or middle class people that want to advance in life and make big efforts to get loans and on top of that pay monthly tuition. We ended up investing about $85,000 for a basically useless degree.

They break their student's spirit, they belittle, ignore, degrade them;  make them feel they are not entitled to any success if they are not the star of the class. My child worked extremely, extremely hard to try to make his dream come true but ended up completely sick and hopeless.

Lastly, teachers steal student's ideas and nothing you can do about it because they have students sign an agreement before beginning of classes. First they tell them that those ideas are worthless, of course.

Look for better options. California is full of excellent and transparent schools, colleges and universities let CCA out, it isn't worth it !!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"J Schaefer","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKHVlG-rueNNHP8G_j8sUOh4qzOdkLAKGd40up-8euDnrynVg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"When I attended back in the late 1970's it was CCAC. Great 2 years of my life ( I attended 2 years at UCSB prior to CCAC) Loved the teachers, administration and knowledge I gained. Had a 35 year career in graphic design following my graduation. It pushed me to excel and develop skills that are highly valued in the industry.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipMedIZPM_XEwXUKPkqhsrL5KdA2H8UNcfD7YocG&fid=0x0:0xf91ee76e3bc440dc"],"When":"2024-10-2"},{"Name":"Jienan Xie","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXRkk5zJvI8-JFN52QlW2cmBnXqwbn9OWp-asEJLLX-K8lLRsBe=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I had the pleasure of attending California Science and Technology University (CSTU), and my experience has been nothing short of exceptional. CSTU offers a unique blend of rigorous academic programs and real-world applications, ensuring that students are not only equipped with theoretical knowledge but also practical skills that are in high demand in today's fast-evolving job market.

**World-Class Faculty**
The professors at CSTU are highly knowledgeable and bring a wealth of industry experience to the classroom. They are dedicated to student success, offering personalized guidance and mentorship. Their teaching methods encourage critical thinking, innovation, and problem-solving, all of which are crucial for careers in technology and business fields.

**Cutting-Edge Curriculum**
The curriculum is well-structured, up-to-date, and focuses on the latest advancements in science, technology, and business management. Whether you're looking to advance your career in IT, data science, or business administration, CSTU has a wide range of programs tailored to equip students with skills relevant to today’s industries.

**Supportive Learning Environment**
One of the highlights of CSTU is the supportive and diverse community. The small class sizes foster a collaborative learning environment where students can engage more closely with their peers and professors. This personalized approach makes a huge difference in enhancing the overall learning experience.

**Career Opportunities**
CSTU places a strong emphasis on professional development. The university offers various career services, including resume building, job placement assistance, and internship opportunities, which are invaluable for students looking to transition into their careers. Many of my peers, including myself, were able to secure rewarding positions in top companies after graduation, thanks to the resources and connections provided by CSTU.

**Convenient Location and Flexible Learning Options**
Located in the heart of Silicon Valley, CSTU is strategically positioned near major tech companies, giving students incredible exposure to the latest industry trends and networking opportunities. The university also offers flexible learning options, including online courses, making it accessible for working professionals.

In conclusion, CSTU provides an enriching, supportive, and innovative learning experience that prepares students to excel in their chosen fields. I highly recommend California Science and Technology University to anyone seeking a top-quality education with a focus on technology, business, and personal growth.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Christine Chen","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKxbbBoo9bmnJWn7nFntCBKTzMz5vFDIJRB6jtOC44Iiu7JpQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"California Science and Technology University (CSTU) offers an ideal learning environment for professionals balancing careers with education. The university provides hands-on machine learning courses and cutting-edge technologies. With a low student-to-professor ratio, CSTU ensures personalized attention, making complex concepts easier to grasp. The expert faculty is dedicated to student success, and the accessible staff fosters a supportive atmosphere. CSTU’s focus on practical application and in-depth understanding makes it a top choice for professionals seeking to stay ahead in the rapidly evolving tech industry.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Lam Ngoc Dao","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWUl02OzbjcpAC0s2dY4hO90paL_-9YyyOZ3bHM3FlZdCPezBgr=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I recently completed the Emerging Technology Training Program and the Master of Science in Computer Systems and Engineering at California Science and Technology University (CSTU), and I couldn't be more impressed with my overall experience!

The admissions process was seamless, and the staff was incredibly helpful and responsive to all of my inquiries. The program offerings are diverse, particularly in tech-related fields such as Machine Learning, Generative AI, Natural Language Processing, Big Data, and Cloud Computing. I appreciate the practical learning experiences integrated into the curriculum, which really prepare students for real-world applications.

The faculty at CSTU demonstrates deep knowledge in their subjects and are always available for academic advising and support. Their commitment to student success is truly commendable.

CSTU has set me up for success in my educational journey.

If you’re considering advancing your education, particularly in the technology and business fields, I highly recommend checking out CSTU!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"高敏","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIwQIm5wGDhc3f2gc8Z5rnr7iWrt-kFXU9MEqfoNNjAaT_50Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"CSTU is a great university that offers a wide variety of cutting-edge courses. [{"Name":"Francis Singh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUEQxOyNfet8378947IgEXoNSiKPs-WFURaCFGkWgbEgTvwAMk=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Its a great school. The professors on campus in the major I was in for my undergraduates were all great. The student live is also good. Made a lot of good friends. The only downside is sometimes the administration sucks. They lack a lot in customer service sometimes.  But they are dedicated and when they work, boy do they work great! I wish there was more fluidity among the admin staff, but they work hard and are under pressure a lot too.

Plan on coming back for a masters program which I am looking forward to. I lived in hayward my whole life, and despite East Bay always being here, it is not a bad school at all.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Ilina Manandhar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWqx_fzfiT-cereurff78VT56pdl7XAED90ePua3uPm3wvnig0=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is my third quarter here in CSU East Bay and I am really loving my experience here. This is a campus that has both diverse students and faculty members. I am a business major and have really enjoyed almost all the classes I have taken here. Most teachers are very helpful and will help you during their office hours/ by email and even through the phone.
Regarding the administration, I have never had any problems with them. I think it depends on you really. If you check you mycsueb regularly it has all the administrative information you need. They have a "to-do" list, with phone numbers to respective departments if you're having trouble. They even send out email as reminders if you're missing a document or something else. You can easily make appointments if you wish to meet with a counselor. Also, they are pretty good about answering your questions through the phone as well..
Overall a very good campus,  at least it has been for me for the past 7 months.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Anastasia Crespo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIZB4uTdAgW_KDnYzC2McGKRdKCo9F5U0guYjTDBeESXYgQjg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I love CSUEB! I've never had much of a problem. I will admit sometimes administration and financial aid can be a pain but classes, professors, the people here have made me love my experience! I also dorm and it isn't too crazy just because it's a commuter campus but i've still had some of the best nights at the dorms. It is what you make it, but there is a lot of fun and good times up on this hill! :)","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Jessica K","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXFRe0zLQmTWtuX9WnUGNRpOhPNMmGR1BQpdAwsGSiWt1F-djCiZg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"So far, I love it <3 I'm in the middle of my fall quarter of college.

There are no real instructions. All you get is a forum and a few preprinted assignments. They tell you to go read a few pages of one of the chapters in the textbook and resolve a deep psychology issue of a woman with abandoning issues and then offer solutions for her troubling marriage. That is, you already need to be an experienced psychoanalyst to be able to do that. As for the teaching you will receive a few links to 5-minute YouTube videos by some of the psychologists out there. If you have issues, your mentor will never get back to you. They demand certain things on the assignments but Whatever you submit they will give you full passing score. This is a good school if you are only after a degree but I hope that public is informed about this issue.","Images":null,"When":""}]
